_comment: MCP servers bundled with the qa plugin — auto-register when the plugin is enabled (no manual project setup). Tools appear namespaced as mcp__plugin_qa_<server>__<tool>. Only secret-free servers are bundled here: figma (design read), playwright (web driver), appium (mobile driver). Lark is NOT bundled — it is read via scripts/lark_read.py (dual tenant/user token from .claude/qa-claude/.plugin.env), because a plugin-bundled MCP server can only read shell env vars, not .plugin.env. Users who still prefer the Lark MCP can add @larksuiteoapi/lark-mcp to their own project .mcp.json (see rules/lark-mcp-guide.md). NOTE: a project/user .mcp.json server with the SAME http endpoint (e.g. figma) takes precedence over this bundle and its tools keep the short name mcp__figma__* — so readers locate figma tools via ToolSearch keyword, not a hardcoded prefix.
_comment_playwright: Launched via scripts/mcp_playwright.py (not npx directly) so HEADLESS can be resolved from the project before the browser server starts. Resolution order: the main project's ./.env (QA_HEADLESS/HEADLESS) → shell env + .plugin.env → default headed. A project that adds its own `playwright` server overrides this bundle entirely and controls headless on its own.
